story:
Across the galaxy, Zephryial Vox—masked in mirrored crystal, voice amplified by smart dust—opens the sixth-season finale of Voice Between Worlds. In seven solars, the once-in-a-century Ecliptic Convocation will convene, and, as always, its venue will be a pocket universe built by a single reality artisan. These artisans—higher-dimensional beings from the Gossamer Spiral—shape space-time itself, but only true masters are trusted to craft realms fit for guests. In ordinary seasons, the victor earns a hundred-year contract with Oblivara Holdings and placement on the Convocation roster; in this rare centennial season, the winner also claims the honor of hosting the approaching Convocation. A recap of last season’s winner, Lieutenant Orrivim, shows his Hourglass Cathedral: a basilica of living sand where time slows around insight and rushes past missteps.

This year pits two rising stars against each other. Soryn Vey, a precise creator of quiet, evocative worlds, presents Evershade, a twilight realm where shared memories unfurl as translucent vignettes whenever guests interact. Old acquaintances see their history; strangers can offer a chosen memory or let an AI—trained on two hundred personality metrics—select one that reveals core values. It promises mystery and, Zephryial teases, headlines. Soryn’s rivals are the twins Thalen and Theryn Thoreux, specialists in duality, with The Mirror Feast. Though born as two, the Thoreux twins are licensed by Oblivara as a single braided artisan consciousness for competition purposes, and their realm lets guests split into two simultaneous selves to dine in parallel: one half sumptuous excess, the other restrained elegance.

The judges enter: Lyra Quendral, “The Guest’s Voice,” practical and precise; the ArchSeer Calyros, a mystical authority; and Drevan Solvane, Oblivara’s immaculate, pen-twirling CEO. They plunge into Evershade, floating down on clouds into an indigo world where silver grass shimmers and forests glow like lanterns. Over Lyra and Calyros, a memory plays—curated or algorithmic, Lyra can’t tell—revealing the ArchSeer’s stark beginnings and softening her impression of him.

Deeper in, Drevan is drawn to groves laced with umbralith, a light-swallowing mineral. A nearby pool reflects not the forest but the beholder’s thoughts. He turns away, refusing introspection. The ArchSeer murmurs that the place knows the shape of his hunger. A memory flares from long ago: Calyros pleading with Drevan to abandon a godlike scheme to resurrect “her.” Drevan bristles: if Orrivim won’t build it, he will find someone who will. The memory fades; present-day Drevan stalks off, furious. Backstage, Soryn is horrified that her world has surfaced such animosity. Zephryial reassures her—seven solars remain to adjust—yet Soryn feels the doom of bad optics. The judges finish, score, and depart.

They step into The Mirror Feast through a stone arch onto an endless sky-mirror. Drevan’s reflection splits; he experiences two paths at once. In the bright half, circular tables bloom with gemstones, florals ride a sweet breeze, and a lava river pours—shockingly heatless—between diners. Playful drinks fizz into colorful hiccups and violet blushes; Lyra giggles, Calyros indulges, and the audience delights. Meanwhile, their mirrored selves cross into the muted half: dusky pastels, a star-bared ceiling, a river of moonlight. Plates arrive with drift-leaf salads, bioluminescent peppers, and a dark mineral sorbet that sends Drevan’s pen into a frenzy. He notes possibilities; efficiency is a word he likes.

Voting begins. The People’s Choice tilts to Soryn Vey with sixty percent—audiences swoon for Evershade’s intimacy (and, Zephryial jokes, for Drevan’s temper). The judges split: Drevan for The Mirror Feast, Calyros for Evershade. A tie. Lyra, weighing comfort, accessibility, and emotional resonance, breaks it for Soryn. Evershade wins season six. Applause swells, but the final beat is caution: Soryn must refine her world—and mend fences with Drevan—before the Convocation assembles inside whatever memories her realm refuses to remember to forget.
